company_type: Manufacturing - Construction and Mining Equipment, Engines, Turbines, Locomotives

supply_chain.dependencies: The company sources raw materials and manufactured components from suppliers both domestically and internationally. These purchases include unformed materials (steel products), rough parts (castings and forgings), and finished parts. The company faces risks from material and component shortages, logistics constraints, and labor inefficiencies that could limit ability to meet customer demand.
supply_chain.risks: Material price increases, fluctuations in demand, significant disruptions to supply chains, significant shortages of labor and material, production challenges at suppliers (including suppliers of semiconductors), disruption in deliveries from suppliers, decreased availability of raw materials or commodities, transportation delays, excess inventories

regulatory_risks: Federal, state and international environmental laws governing use, transport and disposal of substances and control of emissions
Air emissions standards applicable to internal combustion engines
Complex labor, tax, customs and other laws in Brazil
Income tax laws in numerous jurisdictions
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and similar foreign anti-corruption laws

Import and export-related laws and regulations (U.S. Customs and Border Protection, Bureau of Industry and Security, Office of Antiboycott Compliance, Directorate of Defense Trade Controls, Office of Foreign Assets Control)
Embargoes and sanctions imposed by U.S. and other governments
Financial services industry regulations including U.S. Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
Data privacy regulations including European Union's General Data Protection Regulation and California Consumer Privacy Act
Changes in tax laws or treaties
New or additional tariffs or quotas

sanction_risks: Embargoes and sanctions restricting or prohibiting sales to specific persons or countries
Iran sanctions (company maintains trademark portfolio in Iran through IPC, with OFAC specific license, payments to Bank Melli which was designated under Executive Order 13224)
